2. 智能路由前置解析支持剥离 leading @ 标记，保留 /slfk|邵莲 旧入口并支持开关回滚。

3. 补齐 command parsing 回归测试，覆盖命中、误触发抑制、白名单与回滚路径。

4. 同步更新配置 schema 与 README 说明，并更新 CHANGELOG 与 metadata 版本至 0.27.0。

commit.message:
define reducers through a unified define_reducer path

Extend define_reducer to support per-call options and blocks, then migrate sum, sort, percentile, and remaining aggregators to declarative reducer specs built on create_reducer.

This removes special-case wrapper plumbing and keeps reduce/create_reducer as the single reducer-construction path.

commit.message:
feat(ui): add live dashboard and issue pages

Summary:
- add an HTML dashboard and per-issue page backed by the
  orchestrator snapshot API
- stream live state over SSE while keeping the JSON API routes
  and webhook endpoints intact
- harden page routing, sort snapshot output for stable rendering,
  and add regression tests for HTML, 404s, and event streams

Rationale:
- provide a usable self-hosted interface without introducing a
  frontend build pipeline or JS framework
- make runtime visibility easier for operators while avoiding
  accidental dashboard fallbacks on unknown routes

Tests:
- gofmt -w .
- GOPROXY=https://proxy.golang.org,direct go test ./...

commit.message:
fix(mobile): upgrade Expo project to SDK 54 (#1)

Summary:
- Upgraded @cedar/mobile from Expo SDK 52 to SDK 54-compatible versions.
- Updated Expo Router, React, React Native, and related runtime packages.
- Regenerated pnpm lockfile to reflect SDK 54 dependency graph.

Rationale:
- Expo Go on iOS only supports the latest SDK line, and the project
  could not be opened while pinned to SDK 52.
- Aligning the mobile workspace to SDK 54 restores compatibility with
  current Expo Go and keeps dependency constraints coherent.

Tests:
- pnpm --filter @cedar/mobile lint
- pnpm --filter @cedar/mobile test
- pnpm --filter @cedar/mobile build

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

feat(release): 发布0.26.0并收敛任务流阶段闭环

1. 收敛多阶段 flow 触发边界：仅任务执行/补充触发，讨论与深度研究显式排除。\n2. 新增阶段产物分类门禁，命中仅文档或仅 .slfk/docs 变更时跳过自测并保留可观测日志。\n3. 重构 PHASE 间上下文与提交策略：移除 hash 依赖，改为上一阶段文件可在当前 commit 查看，并收敛为每个 PHASE 一次收尾提交。\n4. 完成提交与通知闭环：有远程自动 push，无远程明确跳过 push 提示，任务完成通知优先触达创建者并回退。\n5. 同步更新 README、测试回归、CHANGELOG 与 metadata 版本到 0.26.0。

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
feat(runtime): bootstrap Jira-backed Symphony Go service

Summary:
- add a self-hosted Go runtime that loads WORKFLOW.md, starts an
  orchestrator, and exposes a JSON status API
- implement Jira Cloud issue polling, raw Jira API tooling, workspace
  lifecycle management, and Codex app-server integration
- add project docs, examples, AGENTS guidance, tests, CI workflow,
  env sample, and local developer helpers

Rationale:
- establish an end-to-end baseline for a Go implementation inspired by
  the Symphony spec instead of keeping only a bootstrap skeleton
- make the project directly usable for self-hosted Jira workflows while
  leaving room to extend the runtime with richer UI and integrations
- document the operational model early so future changes stay aligned
  with workspace safety and runtime layering

Tests:
- GOPROXY=https://proxy.golang.org,direct go test ./...
- go run ./cmd/symphonyd
- curl http://127.0.0.1:8080/api/v1/state
- curl -X POST http://127.0.0.1:8080/api/v1/refresh

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
fix(web-fetch): Harden SSRF guards and remove duplicate fetch

Block additional private IPv6 forms, normalize bracketed IPv6 hosts, and pin
hostname fetches to validated DNS results to reduce rebinding risk.

Also reuse the first non-image response in the web-fetch tool path so text
content is not fetched twice.

Add focused unit tests covering IPv6 guard edge cases and single-fetch
behavior for non-image responses.

Co-Authored-By: GPT-5 Codex <codex@openai.com>

Align 2/3-parser options overloads with the precise mode/value/state
style used by other fixed-arity overloads, and rename the 16-parser
regression test to describe the arity-guard behavior directly.

commit.message:
fix(security): harden API abuse controls and clear vuln findings

Summary:
- Add in-memory rate-limit middleware with per-IP and per-user keys.
- Apply route-specific throttles across auth, shared, documents, and
  settings API surfaces.
- Configure HTTP server read/write/header/idle timeouts and max header
  bytes to reduce slow-client resource exhaustion.
- Upgrade backend Go toolchain target to 1.26.1 and patch vulnerable
  Go/JWT/OpenTelemetry dependencies.
- Align backend Docker/CI Go versions with 1.26.1 and add pnpm
  overrides to remediate vulnerable npm transitive dependencies.

Rationale:
- The API previously had no request throttling and no explicit server
  timeout/header limits, leaving it vulnerable to abuse and DDoS-style
  cost amplification.
- Security scans identified known vulnerabilities in npm transitive
  packages and backend runtime/dependency versions; upgrading and
  pinning resolves those findings in both development and CI/deploy
  paths.

Tests:
- pnpm audit --audit-level=high
- pnpm audit
- (cd backend && GOCACHE="$PWD/../.cache/go-build" GOTOOLCHAIN=go1.26.1
  ../.tmp/go/bin/go test ./...)
- (cd backend && GOCACHE="$PWD/../.cache/go-build" GOTOOLCHAIN=go1.26.1
  ../.tmp/go/bin/go run golang.org/x/vuln/cmd/govulncheck@latest ./...)
- pnpm test (fails pre-existing in @speed-reader/api-client: no test files)

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

simplify top-level splitter with expected-closer stack

Track expected closing delimiters directly in the pipeline splitter, improving readability while preserving top-level >> detection and delimiter mismatch/unclosed checks.

Add parser tests covering mismatched and unclosed delimiter errors.

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
fix(cli): Unify dispatch and harden init safety

Route both published and dev CLI entrypoints through one shared dispatcher so command parsing and usage behavior stay consistent.

Avoid eager snapshot module loading for non-snapshot commands to remove unnecessary plugin-load side effects, and reject unsafe init targets by refusing non-directory and non-empty paths.

Add focused CLI tests for dispatch contracts and init safety checks, and include the shared dispatcher in the build output.

- exclude build artifacts and unrelated parent repository history

feat(cli): add item add --root for traits

Support creating project-level trait items via `kspec item add --root --type trait` without requiring manual kynetic.yaml edits. Reuse a shared manifest trait insertion helper so item add, trait add, and plan import follow the same write path, and cover the new contract with CLI tests.

feat: expand semantic indexing and managed project workflows

Add Drupal and Symfony adapters with project type detection, improve managed remote project caching and branch scan execution, and extend semantic indexing for Twig, SDC, Drupal libraries, and YAML-backed relations so the UI and search flows can link assets, services, and project symbols more accurately.

Key improvements:
- Improved SDC component indexing with automatic asset linking (Twig, CSS, JS).
- Added Drupal 7 support to VersionDetector via .info file parsing.
- Enhanced branch management UI with last scan status and rescan actions.
- Fixed ImportCommand to correctly parse DMU upgrade patterns.
- Modernized test suite with PHP 8 attributes and tighter Twig grammar requirements.
- Optimized remote project storage by moving repositories to .cache/ directory.

commit.message:
feat(extension): add reader-mode ingest for full-page speed read

Summary:
- switched the page context-menu label to `Speed Read entire page`
- upgraded full-page extraction to use `@mozilla/readability` with
  selector fallbacks and boilerplate/ad filtering
- propagated extracted page title/source into document creation so
  generated docs open with cleaner metadata in reader mode
- added runtime proof media for parser output at
  `docs/pr-media/SPE-10/parser-proof.png`

Rationale:
- the prior full-page path relied on simple selector/body extraction,
  which frequently included footer/ad/copyright noise
- reader-mode parsing improves extraction quality while keeping a safe
  fallback path when parsing cannot produce article text

Tests:
- pnpm --filter @speed-reader/extension build
- Playwright runtime parser proof on fixture page (boilerplate check)

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

test: annotate validate drift coverage

Add AC annotations for the validate --drift spec and cover file-extension false-positive handling.

Task: @01KHF2BNVZSQV12C2DRK57C5P2
Spec: @validate-drift
Co-Authored-By: OpenAI Codex <codex@openai.com>

feat: derive task deps from spec dependencies

Resolve direct spec depends_on relationships during derive by linking to active derived tasks when they exist, and warn without blocking when they do not. Adds regression coverage for both paths while preserving existing parent/child dependency behavior.

Task: @01KGVGSH49ACMXJBGCYW12ZDYF
Spec: @cmd-derive
Co-Authored-By: OpenAI Codex <codex@openai.com>

fix(settings): harden error typing and settings copy behavior

Summary:
- replace fragile settings handler error routing with typed errors via
  `ErrUserNotFound` and `ValidationError`
- switch pause multiplier updates to per-field pointer semantics so omitted
  values are distinct from explicit values
- align settings copy with runtime behavior and add backend/web tests plus
  runtime media artifacts for validation

Rationale:
- string matching on error text is brittle and can silently misroute status
  codes when upstream wording changes
- pause multiplier updates should not rely on `0.0` as a missing sentinel
  because explicit values and omission are different API intents
- UI copy should match how font size is applied immediately while other
  settings are primarily defaults for new documents

Tests:
- cd backend && "$(go env GOPATH)/bin/go1.24.0" test ./internal/settings
- pnpm --filter @speed-reader/web prebuild
- pnpm --filter @speed-reader/web test -- --run SettingsView
- runtime walkthrough media captured in docs/pr-media/SPE-6

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

Co-Authored-By: Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
fix(extension): harden webstore build and packaging readiness

Summary:
- Added an extension prebuild step that compiles required workspace
  packages before running the extension build.
- Added a deterministic extension package script that zips the built
  dist output using the manifest version in the filename.
- Removed external Google Fonts links from the sidepanel HTML and added
  a Chrome Web Store readiness matrix with policy/source mapping.

Rationale:
- The extension build failed from a clean workspace because shared
  package artifacts were missing, which blocked a reliable release path.
- Production submission requires a reproducible upload artifact and clear
  requirement coverage for Web Store review fields and policy checks.
- Avoiding external font URLs reduces review/runtime risk under MV3
  remote code expectations.

Tests:
- pnpm --filter @speed-reader/types clean && pnpm --filter
  @speed-reader/tokenizer clean && pnpm --filter @speed-reader/engine
  clean && pnpm --filter @speed-reader/api-client clean && pnpm
  --filter @speed-reader/ui clean
- pnpm --filter @speed-reader/extension build
- pnpm --filter @speed-reader/extension package
- ZIP/manifest sanity check for generated package contents

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

Make worksheet regeneration dynamic, regenerate .tst files (#329)

Detect worksheet inputs by scanning tst/worksheets for non-empty
.sheet directories instead of hardcoding a short list. This lets
regen_tests.g create or refresh expected output for all runnable
worksheet fixtures.

Print a brief progress line before each worksheet so long
regeneration runs show which sheet is being processed.

Also let regen_test.g regenerate the .tst files as well.

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>
